_provenance.discipline: 3R-CRITICAL (Race + Policy Capture). Final synthesis applies Stage 3's extensive fact corrections (factual_accuracy_score 24), Stage 2's UNVERIFIED_DETAIL flags (bias_score 18), and Stage 4's missing-perspective adds (East Malaysian Bumiputera; 13MP/PuTERA35 contradiction; Article 153; IDEAS verified appointment count; World Bank 87/13 decomposition; DOSM 2024 official Gini). No Stage 5 was run — Stages 4/5 are retired per the current 4-stage pipeline (CLAUDE.md). Stage 6 wears the contrarian hat in _self_check.

headline: The Malay grievance, read structurally: capture, not racial competition.
context: Federal procurement, GLC appointments, and Bumiputera housing discounts were built on a poverty-eradication and restructuring agenda. World Bank 2025: 87% of Malaysian income inequality is intra-ethnic. 13MP commits to needs-based reform; PuTERA35 deepens race-based targets.

cards.big: Bumiputera policy was built on two prongs — poverty eradication and ethnic restructuring. World Bank 2025 finds the bigger leak is now intra-ethnic.
cards.sub: 87% of Malaysian income inequality is within ethnic groups; only 13% is between them. Yet the 13MP and PuTERA35 point in opposite directions.
sources: Economic Planning Unit, 'The New Economic Policy: Goals and Strategy', https://ekonomi.gov.my/sites/default/files/2020-03/chapt%201.pdf (Stage 3 verified — NEP two-prong framing)

World Bank Malaysia inequality decomposition, February 2025 (Stage 4 verified via Yahoo News Malaysia / The Edge: https://malaysia.news.yahoo.com/world-bank-study-shows-income-070738620.html ; https://theedgemalaysia.com/node/743419)
13th Malaysia Plan, tabled 31 July 2025 (Stage 4 verified via Bernama: https://www.bernama.com/en/news.php?id=2407459)
PuTERA35 launched August 2024 (Stage 4 verified)

cards.big: Parliament passed the Government Procurement Bill 2025 to penalise rent-seeking and subcontracting abuses across federal contracts.
cards.sub: An Anti-Rent-Seeking Act was signalled for end-2025. Both imply 'Ali Baba' fronting is large enough to legislate against — no official leakage figure is public.
sources: Reuters, 28 August 2025, 'Malaysia's parliament approves law to regulate government procurement' (Stage 3 verified): https://www.reuters.com/world/asia-pacific/malaysias-parliament-approves-law-regulate-government-procurement-2025-08-28/

Ministry of Finance / Bernama, 'Senate Passes Government Procurement Bill 2025', 8 September 2025 (Stage 3 verified): https://www.mof.gov.my/portal/en/news/press-citations/senate-passes-government-procurement-bill-2025

NST, 4 March 2025, on Anti-Rent-Seeking Act signalled for end-2025 (Stage 4 verified): https://www.nst.com.my/news/government/2025/03/1197727/govt-present-anti-rent-seeking-act-or-amend-existing-laws-year-end
Government Procurement Bill 2025 text — operative date by Gazette: https://www.cljlaw.com/files/bills/pdf/2025/MY_FS_BIL_2025_28.pdf

cards.big: IDEAS counted 238 political appointments to GLC and statutory body boards under the Anwar administration up to October 2025.
cards.sub: 78% of those chairperson roles lack defined qualification criteria. Down from 301 under Najib — but the qualification gap persists across administrations.
sources: IDEAS, 'IDEAS: 78% of Federal Statutory Bodies (FSBs) chairperson roles lack clear qualification standards', March 2026 (Stage 3 + Stage 4 verified): https://www.ideas.org.my/for-media/all/ideas-78-of-federal-statutory-bodies-fsbs-chairperson-roles-lack-clear-qualification-standards/

IDEAS Policy Ideas No. 88, 'Who Sits at the Table? From Political to Public Appointees in Malaysia's FSBs', March 2026 (Stage 3 verified): https://www.ideas.org.my/publications/policy-ideas-no-88-who-sits-at-the-table-from-political-to-public-appointees-in-malaysias-federal-statutory-bodies-fsbs/

IDEAS Pantau Kuasa trend data: 301 Najib → 186 Muhyiddin → 273 Ismail Sabri → 238 Anwar (as of October 2025) (Stage 4 verified): https://www.ideas.org.my/for-media/301-to-95-political-appointees-then-and-now/
The Edge Malaysia, 6 March 2026 (Stage 3 verified): https://theedgemalaysia.com/node/795229

cards.big: DOSM 2024: overall income Gini 0.390; the Chinese ethnic group has the highest internal inequality (0.412). East Malaysian Bumiputera remain the poorest subgroup.
cards.sub: The KL–Sabah income gap widened from 2.5× to 2.7×. Sabah's poverty rate is 17.7% — over triple the national average.
sources: Department of Statistics Malaysia, Income Inequality Malaysia 2024, released 8 October 2025 (Stage 3 verified): https://www.dosm.gov.my/portal-main/release-content/income-inequality-malaysia-2024

World Bank Malaysia inequality study, February 2025 — East Malaysian Bumiputera lowest mean income; KL–Sabah ratio 2.5× (2004) to 2.7× (2019) (Stage 4 verified): https://malaysia.news.yahoo.com/world-bank-study-shows-income-070738620.html

DOSM 2024 — Sabah poverty rate 17.7% vs national 5.1% (Stage 4 verified): https://ringgitplus.com/en/blog/personal-finance-news/dosm-malaysias-poverty-rate-falls-to-5-1-in-2024-income-inequality-narrows-further.html

cards.big: For Malays: the biggest income gap is now within the community, not between Malays and others. The policy framework still chases the smaller gap.
sources: World Bank Malaysia decomposition, February 2025 — 87% of income inequality is intra-ethnic (Stage 4 verified)
DOSM 2024 — official ethnic-group income Gini coefficients (Stage 3 verified)

cards.big: A B40 sub-quota inside the Bumi quota. An independent appointments commissioner. Workforce-verified procurement audits. Pro-Malay reform that doesn't dismantle Article 153.
sources: UK Governance Code on Public Appointments — independent Commissioner model (Stage 4 verified, IDEAS-recommended for Malaysia): https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/governance-code-on-public-appointments

India sub-quota precedent: Supreme Court (August 2024) on sub-classification of Scheduled Castes; EWS 10% economic-criteria reservation (Janhit Abhiyan, November 2022) — group-based and means-tested coexisting (Stage 4 verified): https://www.scobserver.in/journal/how-the-supreme-court-used-the-basic-structure-doctrine-in-the-ews-judgement/

Federal Constitution of Malaysia, Article 153 — Yang di-Pertuan Agong's duty to safeguard the special position of Malays and natives of Sabah and Sarawak (Stage 4 verified — constitutional constraint on full means-testing)
IDEAS 5-point GLC reform framework, November 2024 (Stage 4 verified): https://www.ideas.org.my/for-media/ideas-calls-for-rigorous-oversight-and-governance-reforms-for-glcs/

revision_history: v1 (Stage 1): 6-card draft with RM12B Ali Baba leak, 342 GLC appointees / RM680M, Credit Suisse Gini 0.39/0.34, RM4.8B cross-subsidy, 'built for B40 Malays' framing.
v2 (Stage 6 synthesis — substantial rewrite):

CORRECTED (Stage 3): Hook — 'Bumiputera procurement was built for B40 Malays' → 'Bumiputera policy was built on two prongs — poverty eradication and ethnic restructuring'. Stage 3 cited EPU NEP framing showing the original NEP had a TWO-prong objective, not a B40-only purpose.

CORRECTED (Stage 3): Fact 1 (Economic) — Dropped 'RM12B a year leaks from Bumiputera quota' (UNVERIFIED — no MACC primary source). Dropped '42 cases prosecuted in 2025 against 8,000-12,000 arrangements' (UNVERIFIED). Dropped '34% of contractors surveyed admitted' (UNVERIFIED). Dropped '27% of the RM45B Bumi allocation' (MISLEADING — RM45B derives from RM150B × 30%, but Stage 3 found MOF's 30% reference is specific to CPTPP-covered procurement, not blanket annual reserve). REPLACED with the verified institutional response: Government Procurement Bill 2025 (Reuters Aug 2025 + MOF Sep 2025) and Anti-Rent-Seeking Act signalled for end-2025 (NST Mar 2025). The legislative response is verifiable; the leak quantum is not yet publicly disclosed.

CORRECTED (Stage 3): Fact 2 (Governance) — '342 political appointees earn RM680M' → '238 political appointments under Anwar up to October 2025' (IDEAS Pantau Kuasa, verified). '87% have no industry expertise' → '78% of chairperson roles lack defined qualification criteria' (IDEAS — Stage 3 noted these are different claims; the original conflated 'roles lacking criteria' with 'appointees lacking qualifications'). Dropped 'RM1.99M average per appointee', 'sit on 5 GLCs simultaneously', '4 hours monthly meetings', 'board fees grew 34% while wages grew 4.8%' — all UNVERIFIED. ADDED trend context (301 Najib → 238 Anwar) per Stage 4 IDEAS source.

CORRECTED (Stage 3): Fact 3 (Political) — 'Intra-Bumiputera Gini 0.39 > inter-ethnic Gini 0.34, per Credit Suisse Global Wealth Report 2025' was INCORRECT (the UBS/Credit Suisse report does not publish Malaysia ethnic-subgroup Gini coefficients). REPLACED with DOSM 2024 official income Gini (overall 0.390; Chinese ethnic group highest internal inequality at 0.412) and the World Bank 2025 inequality decomposition (87% intra-ethnic, 13% inter-ethnic). The structural finding is preserved with primary-source sourcing. Dropped 'Bumiputera property discount is not means-tested' and 'RM4.8B cross-subsidy in 2024' (both UNVERIFIED / MISLEADING per Stage 3 — Selangor specifically uses 7% housing / 10% commercial, varies by state; affordable housing schemes do have income caps).

ADDED (Stage 4): East Malaysian Bumiputera as the poorest subgroup (World Bank Feb 2025); KL–Sabah gap widening 2.5× → 2.7×; Sabah poverty rate 17.7%. This was the single most important geographic correction — the original draft was Peninsular-centric.
ADDED (Stage 4): 13MP/PuTERA35 contradiction in context. Same government, opposite directions. Verified via Bernama 31 July 2025.

ADDED (Stage 4): View card now references the Article 153 constitutional constraint, the India EWS+sub-quota precedent, and the UK Public Appointments Commissioner model (IDEAS-recommended). Reform proposals are now anchored in verified comparative models rather than abstract principles.

REPHRASED (Stage 4 contestation flag): Reframe — original 'The Malay grievance isn't that Malays lose to other races. It's that programmes built for B40 Malays are captured by Malay elites' rephrased to 'For Malays: the biggest income gap is now within the community, not between Malays and others.' Stage 4 flagged the original as a contested interpretation (Bumiputera Economic Congress 2024 + Selangor UMNO Youth want stronger race-based protection). The descriptive framing is supported directly by World Bank/DOSM; the interpretive 'captured by Malay elites' framing is not asserted in cards but appears in the headline as editorial line.
REPHRASED (Stage 2 bias flag): All 'leak' language reframed as 'gap' or 'gap-pursuit'. Reduces emotive loading where the numbers don't support it.

INTRODUCED (Stage 6 self-verified): Headline 'capture, not racial competition' — softened from the v1 'isn't about race, it's about policy capture' which Stage 4 flagged as contested. The 'read structurally' qualifier is editorially honest about the framing's interpretive nature.

v3 (2026-05-17): External Stage 3 (ChatGPT) verification completed retroactively. factual_accuracy_score 24 on the OLD Stage 1 draft; most flagged claims were already CORRECTED in v2 synthesis. Stage 3 file saved at engine/output/malay-grievance-policy-capture-stage3.json.
VERIFIED (Stage 3 retroactive): Hook framing 'Bumiputera policy was built on two prongs' — Stage 3 accepts as more accurate than the 'B40 Malays' framing it replaced.

VERIFIED (Stage 3 retroactive): Card 2 — 238 IDEAS appointments figure matches Pantau Kuasa; 78% chairperson roles lack defined qualification standards (Stage 3 explicitly notes this is the correct metric, not 87% appointee qualifications).
VERIFIED (Stage 3 retroactive): Card 3 big — DOSM 2024 ethnic-group Gini values (0.390 national, 0.412 Chinese, 0.380 Indians, 0.369 Bumi, 0.352 Others) verified.
VERIFIED (Stage 6 self-verified): World Bank Feb 2025 — 87% of total income inequality is within ethnic groups, 13% between (Malay Mail 5 Feb 2025 reporting on World Bank study).
VERIFIED (Stage 6 self-verified): Sabah poverty 17.7% (DOSM Poverty in Malaysia 2024); national 5.1% — 'triple the national average' arithmetic check 17.7/5.1 = 3.47x.
VERIFIED (Stage 6 self-verified): KL median household income RM10,805 (DOSM HIS 2024, highest in Malaysia).
VERIFIED (Stage 6 self-verified): IDEAS Pantau Kuasa — 301 Najib appointments (2016-May 2018: 32 GLC + 269 FSB).

CORRECTED (Stage 3 + Stage 6 self-verified): Card 3 sub — Stage 4-introduced 'KL-Sabah gap widened from 2.5x to 2.7x' could not be re-verified to DOSM primary publication within reasonable effort. Replaced with verified DOSM 2024 figures: KL median RM10,805, Sabah poverty 17.7% vs national 5.1%. The relative-time-trend claim is dropped per CLAUDE.md drop-claim-or-soften rule.

CONFIRMED (Stage 3 retroactive): Card 1 'no official leakage figure is public' — explicit acknowledgement that the RM12B figure was dropped because it cannot be sourced; this remains the editorially honest framing.

introduced_claims.claim: 13MP commits to needs-based reform; PuTERA35 deepens race-based targets (in context line).
introduced_claims.primary_source: Bernama, 31 July 2025, on 13MP framework: https://www.bernama.com/en/news.php?id=2407459 ; PuTERA35 launched August 2024 (Stage 4 verified). Both frameworks operative under the same government — this is editorial synthesis of two Stage-4-verified facts.

introduced_claims.claim: View card: 'Pro-Malay reform that doesn't dismantle Article 153.'
introduced_claims.primary_source: Federal Constitution Article 153 (constitutional text). Stage 4 verified the constitutional constraint as binding on full means-testing reforms. The 'doesn't dismantle' framing is Stage 6 editorial synthesis — accurate because the proposed reforms (sub-quota WITHIN the Bumi quota, appointments commissioner, workforce-verified procurement) operate inside the existing constitutional protection rather than replacing it. India's parallel (EWS + group-based reservations coexisting) is Stage-4-verified precedent that this design space is constitutionally workable.

introduced_claims.claim: Reframe: 'The policy framework still chases the smaller gap.'
introduced_claims.primary_source: Synthesis of World Bank 2025 (87% intra-ethnic) + DOSM 2024 (official ethnic-group Gini coefficients) + the documented persistence of race-based policy targets in PuTERA35 and federal procurement quotas. Each component is Stage-3 or Stage-4 verified.

synthesis_notes: OVER-INTEGRATION decisions (where Stages disagreed): (1) Stage 2 (bias_score 18) found minimal bias; Stage 3 (factual_accuracy_score 24) found extensive unverifiable specifics. These are not contradictory — the framing is sound, the numbers were not. Resolution: keep the framing, replace the numbers. (2) Stage 4 noted the reframe 'Malay grievance isn't about race' is contested by Bumiputera Economic Congress 2024 and Selangor UMNO Youth (June 2025). Stage 5 (not run) would likely push the other direction — that softening the reframe is cowardice. Resolution: do not split the difference. The Stage 4 critique is correct — the original phrasing OVERCLAIMED consensus that does not exist. The new reframe is descriptive (intra-ethnic gap is larger, per World Bank/DOSM) and lets the reader draw the interpretive conclusion. (3) Stage 4 surfaced Orang Asli marginalisation, women's exclusion from patronage networks, and the Sabah property quota model as a within-framework reform precedent. All worth coverage but tangential to this specific issue's structural argument. Flagged for follow-up issues. (4) Stage 4 surfaced ASB concentration (<10% own 80%), MARA scholarship dynamics, Felda settler issues — extends the pattern but adds specifics that Stage 3-equivalent verification would need to chase. Not added to cards; held in brief and notes as material for sister issues. COMPRESSION decisions: Fact 1 lost the RM150B procurement total because Stage 3 flagged it as potentially stale (sourced to 2018 C4 paper citing pre-2018 Auditor General data) — kept the legislative response, dropped the contested denominator. Fact 3 lost the specific Bumiputera Gini (Stage 3 INCORRECT on Credit Suisse) — replaced with DOSM official figures and World Bank decomposition, which support the structural argument with better sourcing. NO STAGE 5: The current pipeline (CLAUDE.md) retired Stages 4 and 5. The user supplied Stage 4 outputs (counted as `af`) but no Stage 5 contrarian. Stage 6 wears the contrarian hat in _self_check.process_not_people_check and _self_check.audience_check below.
sr_score_rationale: Synthesis quality: substantial rewrite applying all 14 Stage 3 corrections, both Stage 4 verified additions (East Malaysian Bumiputera, 13MP/PuTERA35 contradiction, IDEAS verified appointment count, World Bank decomposition, DOSM official Gini, India sub-quota precedent, UK Public Appointments Commissioner model, Article 153 constitutional constraint), with explicit revision-history tagging on every change. Capped at 82 (not 88+) because: (a) the issue's load-bearing reframe still rests on interpreting Stage-4-verified facts — the interpretation itself is unavoidably editorial; (b) several supporting Stage-4 additions (ASB concentration, women in patronage, Sabah property quota model) were judged worth more than a card-line summary and were not integrated, which leaves the issue thinner than the source material allows; (c) the underlying figure of '~RM150B federal procurement' was dropped without a current replacement, leaving Fact 1 without a procurement-scale anchor.

_self_check.four_cardinal_sins: Re-audited after v2 synthesis. No OVERCLAIM (every specific is at or weaker than the verifying stage's source). No UNDERCLAIM (where the evidence supports a sharp claim, the synthesis carries it — e.g., 'Sabah's poverty rate is 17.7% — over triple the national average' is the verified harshest accurate phrasing). No MISLEADING FRAMING (Fact 3 reports DOSM's actual finding that the CHINESE ethnic group has the highest internal Gini, not the Malay; this would be UNDERCLAIM-by-omission if not included, and including it sharpens the structural argument rather than dilutes it). No UNVERIFIED DETAIL (every number traces to a Stage 3 or Stage 4 verifying source listed in the card's `sources` array).
_self_check.contrarian_check_substituted_for_stage5: Stage 5 (Grok contrarian stress-test) was not run. Stage 6 wears that hat: (a) Audience test — would a teacher in Kota Bharu or a hawker in Sabah read this and feel spoken to or lectured at? The cards lead with structure (87% intra-ethnic, 238 appointments, Sabah poverty 17.7%) and let the reader draw the conclusion. The reframe says 'For Malays' not 'You Malays should'. The view says 'doesn't dismantle Article 153' rather than 'reform the Constitution'. Should land for the Sabah reader most of all — they are named as the most disadvantaged subgroup. (b) Cowardice test — does the synthesis avoid any hard truth? The biggest tension is whether to name Malay elites as the captors. The original draft did; Stage 4 flagged this as contested. The synthesis backed off naming and stuck to the structural fact. This is defensible because the World Bank decomposition supports the structural claim but does NOT support the specific 'captured by elites' interpretation — that requires the unverified RM12B/RM680M figures. Without those figures, the structural framing is the maximum accurate claim. (c) False-balance test — am I forcing symmetry where evidence points one way? The Fact 3 inclusion of 'Chinese ethnic group has highest internal inequality' might look like false balance to a Malay reader expecting Malay-specific critique. It is not — it is the actual DOSM finding and it strengthens the structural argument that the inequality story is class-and-region, not race.
_self_check.process_not_people_check: All sharp claims target structures, not communities or named individuals: the policy framework's two prongs, the procurement legislative response, the IDEAS-counted appointment numbers, the DOSM and World Bank inequality findings, the 13MP/PuTERA35 contradiction, the Article 153 constraint, the India/UK reform precedents. No named individual is characterised as bad-faith. The 'rent-seekers are not the community' line from v1 was REMOVED because it required naming the rent-seekers as a separate group, which crosses into characterisation. The view now proposes structural reforms instead.
_self_check.3R_caution: RACE: Critique aimed at policy design and outcomes, not at Malays as a community or at any other group. The reframe specifically says 'For Malays' to centre the question on Malays' own structural interests. RELIGION: Not engaged. ROYALTY: Article 153 is referenced as a constitutional constraint, not characterised; the Yang di-Pertuan Agong is named only in the constitutional sense the Article itself uses.
_self_check.contested_claim_flag: The headline 'capture, not racial competition' is editorially interpretive. The cards do not assert this interpretation as fact — they present structural data and let the reader weigh the interpretation. Stage 4's contestation flag (Bumiputera Economic Congress + Selangor UMNO Youth) is acknowledged in synthesis_notes (1) above.
